overview: The leave management system follows a microservices architecture with four core components: a React-based frontend web application, a backend leave-service API for managing leave requests and approvals, a user-service API for employee/manager relationships and leave balances, and a lead-service API for managing employee-lead assignments. The frontend communicates with all backend services via REST APIs, while services use in-memory storage for simplicity. All components run as Docker containers on Kubernetes with health monitoring.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests specifying start date, end date, and reason
Managers must be able to view all pending leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or reject leave requests with optional comments
Employees must be able to view the status of all their submitted leave requests
All users must be able to view their current leave balance and annual entitlement
The system must track leave request states: pending, approved, rejected
The system must maintain employee-manager relationships to filter requests appropriately
All services must expose a /health endpoint for readiness checks
The frontend must provide an intuitive UI for both employee and manager workflows
Leave balances must be updated when requests are approved
Employees must be able to type and add a name as a lead for themselves
The system must store and retrieve employee-lead assignments
Lead assignments must be independent of the manager hierarchy

components.name: leave-web-app
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management Web App
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend application for leave management
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Application is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a React-based single-page application using TypeScript and Create React App or Vite. Port 3000. Use React Router for navigation. Provide two main views: Employee View with a form to submit leave requests (start date, end date, reason text area), a table showing all user's requests with status badges (pending/approved/rejected), and a card displaying current leave balance and entitlement by calling GET /user-service/api/v1/users/{userId}/balance. Manager View displays a table of pending requests from direct reports fetched via GET /leave-service/api/v1/requests?managerId={id}&status=pending, with approve/reject buttons that call PUT /leave-service/api/v1/requests/{id}/approve or /reject. Use fetch or axios for API calls to leave-service on port 9090 and user-service on port 9091. Handle loading states and error messages gracefully. Implement basic authentication simulation with a userId stored in localStorage or context. Style with a modern CSS framework like Tailwind or Material-UI. Include a navigation bar to switch between employee and manager views. Implement a /health endpoint that returns {status: ok}. Use environment variables for service URLs. Dockerfile: multi-stage build with node:18-alpine, install dependencies, build static assets, serve with nginx:alpine. Expose port 3000. Include .dockerignore for node_modules and build artifacts.

components.name: leave-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: API for managing leave requests and approvals
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
    description: Local development server
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/v1/requests:
    get:
      summary: List leave requests
      parameters:
        - name: userId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Filter by employee user ID
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Filter by manager user ID
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
          description: Filter by request status
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
    post:
      summary: Create a new leave request
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - userId
                - startDate
                - endDate
                - reason
              properties:
                userId:
                  type: string
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Leave request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid input
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
  /api/v1/requests/{id}/approve:
    put:
      summary: Approve a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                comment:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request approved
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request or manager not authorized
        '404':
          description: Request not found
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
  /api/v1/requests/{id}/reject:
    put:
      summary: Reject a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                comment:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request rejected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request or manager not authorized
        '404':
          description: Request not found
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
components:
  schemas:
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        userId:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        reason:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
        managerId:
          type: string
        managerComment:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go REST API using net/http on port 9090. Use an in-memory map to store leave requests with generated UUIDs as keys. Define structs: LeaveRequest with fields Id, UserId, StartDate, EndDate, Reason, Status (pending/approved/rejected), ManagerId, ManagerComment, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt. On startup, seed with sample data for testing. Implement GET /health returning {status: ok}. GET /api/v1/requests supports query params userId, managerId, status for filtering. When managerId is provided, call GET http://user-service:9091/api/v1/users/{managerId}/reports to get list of direct report userIds, then filter requests to only those from reports. POST /api/v1/requests validates input (dates, userId), calls user-service GET /api/v1/users/{userId} to verify user exists and fetch their managerId, creates request with status=pending, stores it. PUT /api/v1/requests/{id}/approve checks that managerId in query param matches the request's managerId (authorization check), updates status to approved, stores optional comment, calls user-service PUT /api/v1/users/{userId}/balance/deduct with days calculated from startDate to endDate. PUT /api/v1/requests/{id}/reject similar but sets status=rejected, no balance deduction. Return 404 if request not found, 400 if validation fails or manager not authorized. Use encoding/json for marshaling. Add CORS middleware to allow requests from frontend. Log all requests. Use context with timeouts for external calls. Dockerfile: FROM golang:1.21-alpine AS builder, WORKDIR /app, COPY go.mod go.sum ./, RUN go mod download, COPY . ., RUN go build -o leave-service. FROM alpine:latest, COPY --from=builder /app/leave-service ., EXPOSE 9090, CMD [./leave-service]. Include go.mod with module name and dependencies.

components.name: user-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: User Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: API for managing users, leave balances, and organizational hierarchy
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9091
    description: Local development server
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/v1/users/{userId}:
    get:
      summary: Get user details
      parameters:
        - name: userId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: User details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/User'
        '404':
          description: User not found
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
  /api/v1/users/{userId}/balance:
    get:
      summary: Get user leave balance
      parameters:
        - name: userId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance information
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveBalance'
        '404':
          description: User not found
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
  /api/v1/users/{userId}/balance/deduct:
    put:
      summary: Deduct days from leave balance
      parameters:
        - name: userId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - days
              properties:
                days:
                  type: number
                  format: float
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Balance updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveBalance'
        '400':
          description: Invalid input or insufficient balance
        '404':
          description: User not found
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
  /api/v1/users/{managerId}/reports:
    get:
      summary: Get list of direct reports for a manager
      parameters:
        - name: managerId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of user IDs who report to this manager
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  type: string
        '404':
          description: Manager not found
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
components:
  schemas:
    User:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        name:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        role:
          type: string
          enum: [employee, manager]
        managerId:
          type: string
    LeaveBalance:
      type: object
      properties:
        userId:
          type: string
        entitlement:
          type: number
          format: float
        used:
          type: number
          format: float
        remaining:
          type: number
          format: float
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go REST API using net/http on port 9091. Use in-memory maps to store users and leave balances. Define structs: User (Id, Name, Email, Role, ManagerId) and LeaveBalance (UserId, Entitlement, Used, Remaining). On startup, seed data with at least 5 employees and 2 managers showing reporting relationships. Each employee has a managerId pointing to their manager. Managers have role=manager. Initialize leave balances with entitlement=20.0, used=0.0, remaining=20.0 for all users. Implement GET /health returning {status: ok}. GET /api/v1/users/{userId} returns user object or 404. GET /api/v1/users/{userId}/balance returns balance object or 404. PUT /api/v1/users/{userId}/balance/deduct accepts {days: float}, validates days > 0 and days <= remaining, subtracts from remaining, adds to used, returns updated balance. Return 400 if insufficient balance or invalid input. GET /api/v1/users/{managerId}/reports iterates users map, filters where ManagerId == managerId, returns array of user IDs. Return empty array if no reports. Use sync.RWMutex for thread-safe map access. JSON encoding/decoding with proper error handling. CORS middleware for cross-origin requests. Dockerfile: multi-stage build with golang:1.21-alpine, compile to static binary, final image alpine:latest, EXPOSE 9091, CMD to run binary. Include go.mod with appropriate module declaration.

components.name: lead-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Lead Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: API for managing employee lead assignments
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9092
    description: Local development server
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/v1/leads:
    post:
      summary: Assign a lead to an employee
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - userId
                - leadName
              properties:
                userId:
                  type: string
                  description: Employee user ID
                leadName:
                  type: string
                  description: Name of the lead
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Lead assignment created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeadAssignment'
        '400':
          description: Invalid input
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
  /api/v1/leads/{userId}:
    get:
      summary: Get lead assignment for an employee
      parameters:
        - name: userId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Lead assignment details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeadAssignment'
        '404':
          description: Lead assignment not found
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
    put:
      summary: Update lead assignment for an employee
      parameters:
        - name: userId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - leadName
              properties:
                leadName:
                  type: string
                  description: New lead name
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Lead assignment updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeadAssignment'
        '400':
          description: Invalid input
        '404':
          description: Lead assignment not found
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
    delete:
      summary: Remove lead assignment for an employee
      parameters:
        - name: userId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Lead assignment removed
        '404':
          description: Lead assignment not found
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
components:
  schemas:
    LeadAssignment:
      type: object
      properties:
        userId:
          type: string
          description: Employee user ID
        leadName:
          type: string
          description: Name of the lead
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go REST API using net/http on port 9092. Use an in-memory map with userId as key to store lead assignments. Define struct LeadAssignment with fields UserId, LeadName, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt. Implement GET /health returning {status: ok}. POST /api/v1/leads accepts userId and leadName, validates both are non-empty strings, creates new assignment with current timestamps, stores in map, returns 201 with assignment object. If userId already has an assignment, return 400 with error message. GET /api/v1/leads/{userId} retrieves assignment from map, returns 200 with object or 404 if not found. PUT /api/v1/leads/{userId} updates leadName for existing assignment, sets UpdatedAt to current time, returns 200 with updated object or 404 if not found. Validates leadName is non-empty. DELETE /api/v1/leads/{userId} removes assignment from map, returns 204 on success or 404 if not found. Use sync.RWMutex for thread-safe map operations. JSON encoding/decoding with proper error handling. CORS middleware to allow cross-origin requests from frontend. Log all incoming requests with method, path, and status code. Dockerfile: multi-stage build using golang:1.21-alpine as builder, WORKDIR /app, COPY go.mod go.sum and download dependencies, COPY source files, RUN go build -o lead-service. Final stage FROM alpine:latest, COPY binary from builder, EXPOSE 9092, CMD to run the binary. Include go.mod with module declaration and standard library dependencies only.
